authorization_boundary.authorization: The repository owner authorizes reuse of their own original code.
does_not_cover: generated code whose inputs or license are not cleared
assets, fonts, trademarks, or data owned by others
authorization_boundary.effect: Authorization is evidence about authorship permission only; it does not replace license compliance or provenance review.
authorization_boundary.per_file_rule: Every file proposed for copying or adaptation must be reviewed at its pinned upstream blob, traced through available history, classified by authorship, checked for embedded or generated material, and recorded before inclusion.
authorization_boundary.unreviewed_rule: A repository-level license or owner authorization does not clear an unreviewed file.

determinism.array_order: entries ordered by id; file arrays ordered by destination path

cleared_files.modification_summary: Removed mutable overrides and HOME/tmp fallback; added explicit root, archive containment, strict signer/team/architecture/layout checks, atomic promotion, and content-free output.

Narrowed to one owner self-chat; added one-record private account/service revalidation, pre-ack generation filtering, authenticated global routing, exact signed proof validation, no-GUID unknown echo recovery, and terminal lease-releasing supervision.

Required explicit private token paths, exactly-one-record chat/account discovery with private disambiguation, content-free status, deterministic controller setup, and verified-load-state service/tool uninstall.
Kept exact owner-only schema while adding the mandatory mode-0600 controller token and realistic opaque Messages account binding.

Retained strict framed RPC and no-resend ambiguity; added bounded generation-scoped pre-ack buffering, activity-fresh readiness, startup restart/stop handling, close/terminate/kill escalation, and terminal restart exhaustion.
Added stable HMAC event idempotency, persisted exact routes/in-flight backoff, GUID-HMAC crash recovery, transactional echo classification, and no-resend legacy ambiguity quarantine.

entries.planned_use: Adapted owner-only iMessage implementation and immutable installer; every source blob and destination is enumerated.
entries.review: Per-file MIT review retained; repaired implementation is tested against the pinned v0.12.3 RPC payload shape and remains a narrowed local adaptation.

entries.planned_use: Execute the official actions/attest-build-provenance workflow action only at its immutable full commit SHA.
Execute the official actions/checkout workflow action only at its immutable full commit SHA.
Execute the official actions/configure-pages workflow action only at its immutable full commit SHA.
Execute the official actions/deploy-pages workflow action only at its immutable full commit SHA.
Execute the official actions/download-artifact workflow action only at its immutable full commit SHA.
Execute the official actions/setup-python workflow action only at its immutable full commit SHA.
Execute the official actions/upload-artifact workflow action only at its immutable full commit SHA.
Execute the official actions/upload-pages-artifact workflow action only at its immutable full commit SHA.
Install only the immutable signed v0.12.3 macOS release after archive hash, strict code signature, Team ID, architecture, and layout verification.
Runtime ECDSA P-256 signing and verification through the exact hashed macOS arm64 CPython 3.11 wheel.
None. The Microsoft upstream pin, not this owner-account copy, is the only candidate runtime baseline.

review.reason: Avoids confusing fork or copied ancestry with the selected upstream. No file has been reviewed for reuse.

entries.planned_use: None. The event-wrapper contract is taken from the pinned MIT neighborhood protocol; the commons implementation is not a source input.

review.reason: Implementation source is unnecessary for this profile and is not covered by the target MIT source policy.

entries.planned_use: Behavioral authority and grail pointer for version 0.6.16 only. No source, archive, generated bundle, or asset may be copied.
Secondary behavioral reference for local operation and the initial iMessage implementation. It is not a source bundle.
Normative behavioral reference for messaging trust, durable delivery, and the iMessage profile. No implementation source is planned for copying.
Normative behavioral reference for the complete twin-chat envelope and signed commons-event wrapper. No implementation source is planned for copying.
Secondary artifact-contract and implementation reference only, and only for material shown by per-file review to be directly owner-authored and authorized.

review.reason: Reference use is authorized only for directly owner-authored material that passes a separate per-file provenance review; the repository contains mixed licensing and is not a source bundle.

entries.planned_use: Behavioral and license comparison for the clean-room runtime only. No source file was copied or adapted.
New clean-room runtime, integration code, tests, schemas, build logic, and documentation authored specifically for the public target.

source_file_provenance.methodology: Exact source scan cross-bound to original/generated project records, adapted OpenRappter blobs, local third-party license copies, and mixed external evidence. Unknown or mixed evidence never receives an inferred attribution.

evidence_inputs.role: audited public-account snapshot; not a source bundle
audit narrative and contradiction inventory; not a source bundle

inclusion_state_definitions.adapted_source: Pinned MIT source was reviewed per file, modified for this profile, attributed, and mapped from exact blob to destination.
inclusion_state_definitions.candidate_source: May supply code only after commit, license, per-file authorship/provenance, destination, and patch review are complete.
inclusion_state_definitions.dependency_locked: External runtime dependency is not vendored and may be installed only from its exact verified source/release evidence.
inclusion_state_definitions.excluded: Must not supply code, assets, binaries, or generated material to this project.
inclusion_state_definitions.original_new: Newly authored specifically for this project and intended for the target MIT license.
inclusion_state_definitions.pointer_only: May establish desired behavior or lead to direct evidence; must never be copied as source.
inclusion_state_definitions.reference_only: May inform contract interpretation; no code or assets may be copied without a later explicit state change and per-file review.

legal_review_scope: Per-file provenance and declared-license mapping verified for included/adapted inputs; this is not an independent external legal audit or legal opinion.

product_policy.release_process: protected unchanged prerelease, public redownload proof, then retain RC or promote the same assets/commit

release_rule: Local packaging trust and protected candidate/postflight tooling are resolved. Final exact public commit, live enrollment, public attestations, released Pages, and downloaded-byte equality remain external gates; candidate assets and source commit may not change between stages.
